tech_chain: MORPHTOINFESTEDTERRAN_INFESTEDTERRANS: [Larva -> Burrow (RESEARCH_BURROW)] + [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> Infestor (LARVATRAIN_INFESTOR)] -> InfestorTerran (BURROWDOWN_INFESTORTERRAN)

MORPHTOINFESTEDTERRAN_INFESTEDTERRANS: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> Infestor (LARVATRAIN_INFESTOR) -> Burrow (RESEARCH_BURROW) -> InfestorBurrowed (BURROWDOWN_INFESTOR)] -> InfestorTerran (BURROWUP_INFESTORTERRAN)

relations.description: Executing MORPHTOINFESTEDTERRAN_INFESTEDTERRANS results in Unit InfestorTerran.

Ability.name: RESEARCH_PHOENIXANIONPULSECRYSTALS
tech_chain: RESEARCH_PHOENIXANIONPULSECRYSTALS: [Pylon (PROTOSSBUILD_PYLON) -> Gateway (PROTOSSBUILD_GATEWAY) -> CyberneticsCore (PROTOSSBUILD_CYBERNETICSCORE) -> Stargate (PROTOSSBUILD_STARGATE) -> FleetBeacon (PROTOSSBUILD_FLEETBEACON)] -> PhoenixRangeUpgrade (RESEARCH_PHOENIXANIONPULSECRYSTALS)

Ability.name: FLEETBEACONRESEARCH_TEMPESTRANGEUPGRADE
tech_chain: FLEETBEACONRESEARCH_TEMPESTRANGEUPGRADE: [Pylon (PROTOSSBUILD_PYLON) -> Gateway (PROTOSSBUILD_GATEWAY) -> CyberneticsCore (PROTOSSBUILD_CYBERNETICSCORE) -> Stargate (PROTOSSBUILD_STARGATE) -> FleetBeacon (PROTOSSBUILD_FLEETBEACON)] -> TempestRangeUpgrade (FLEETBEACONRESEARCH_TEMPESTRANGEUPGRADE)

relations.description: Executing FLEETBEACONRESEARCH_TEMPESTRANGEUPGRADE results in Upgrade TempestRangeUpgrade.
Executing FLEETBEACONRESEARCH_RESEARCHVOIDRAYSPEEDUPGRADE results in Upgrade VoidRaySpeedUpgrade.
Executing FLEETBEACONRESEARCH_TEMPESTRESEARCHGROUNDATTACKUPGRADE results in Upgrade TempestGroundAttackUpgrade.

Ability.name: FUNGALGROWTH_FUNGALGROWTH
tech_chain: FUNGALGROWTH_FUNGALGROWTH: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> Infestor (LARVATRAIN_INFESTOR)] -> FUNGALGROWTH_FUNGALGROWTH (FUNGALGROWTH_FUNGALGROWTH)
description: Fungal Growth restricts movement, prevents affected units from entering transport units, inhibits the Stalker's Blink and Battlecruiser's Tactical Jump abilities, reveals Cloaked and Burrowed units for its duration, does not stack, and has no upper limit on potential targets.
Fungal Growth immobilizes and kills Marines and Medivacs, mitigates the mobility of Bio units, and protects Brood Lords from Marines and Vikings.

Ability.name: GUARDIANSHIELD_GUARDIANSHIELD
description: Create a shield bubble around the Sentry that reduces the damage of all incoming ranged attacks by 2 for 15 seconds.
Reduces incoming ranged attack damage by 2 per hit for all friendly units inside a large radius around the Sentry for 11 seconds.

Ability.name: EFFECT_REPAIR_MULE
description: Repair some unit or strcture.

Ability.name: NEXUSTRAINMOTHERSHIP_MOTHERSHIP
tech_chain: NEXUSTRAINMOTHERSHIP_MOTHERSHIP: [Pylon (PROTOSSBUILD_PYLON) -> Gateway (PROTOSSBUILD_GATEWAY) -> CyberneticsCore (PROTOSSBUILD_CYBERNETICSCORE) -> Stargate (PROTOSSBUILD_STARGATE) -> FleetBeacon (PROTOSSBUILD_FLEETBEACON)] + [Nexus (PROTOSSBUILD_NEXUS)] -> Mothership (NEXUSTRAINMOTHERSHIP_MOTHERSHIP)

relations.description: Using NEXUSTRAINMOTHERSHIP_MOTHERSHIP requires the prerequisite unit or structure FleetBeacon.
Executing NEXUSTRAINMOTHERSHIP_MOTHERSHIP results in Unit Mothership.

Ability.name: FEEDBACK_FEEDBACK
tech_chain: FEEDBACK_FEEDBACK: [Pylon (PROTOSSBUILD_PYLON) -> Gateway (PROTOSSBUILD_GATEWAY) -> CyberneticsCore (PROTOSSBUILD_CYBERNETICSCORE) -> TwilightCouncil (PROTOSSBUILD_TWILIGHTCOUNCIL) -> TemplarArchive (PROTOSSBUILD_TEMPLARARCHIVE) -> HighTemplar (GATEWAYTRAIN_HIGHTEMPLAR)] -> FEEDBACK_FEEDBACK (FEEDBACK_FEEDBACK)
description: Deals pure damage that ignores all types of armor to enemy spell casters, and can decloak Banshees even if the attack fails to kill.

Ability.name: EFFECT_MASSRECALL_STRATEGICRECALL
tech_chain: EFFECT_MASSRECALL_STRATEGICRECALL: [Pylon (PROTOSSBUILD_PYLON) -> Gateway (PROTOSSBUILD_GATEWAY) -> CyberneticsCore (PROTOSSBUILD_CYBERNETICSCORE) -> Stargate (PROTOSSBUILD_STARGATE) -> FleetBeacon (PROTOSSBUILD_FLEETBEACON) -> Mothership (NEXUSTRAINMOTHERSHIP_MOTHERSHIP)] -> EFFECT_MASSRECALL_STRATEGICRECALL (EFFECT_MASSRECALL_STRATEGICRECALL)
description: Instantly teleport the unit back to its current location.
Recalls the Mothership to any Nexus on the map and brings any nearby units with it.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_ARCHON
description: Create a fake Archon to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_COLOSSUS
description: Create a fake Colossus to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_HIGHTEMPLAR
description: Create a fake High Templar to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.
Hallucinations associated with certain tech trees, such as High Templar, can be presented to the enemy to try instigate inappropriate reactionary counterplay.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_IMMORTAL
description: Create a fake Immortal to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.
Creates a Hallucinated Immortal that can be presented to the enemy to try instigate inappropriate reactionary counterplay.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_PHOENIX
description: Create a fake Phoenix to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.
Creates Hallucinated Phoenixes to fly across the map to look at the enemy's base or locate the enemy's army.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_PROBE
description: Create a fake Probe to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_STALKER
description: Create a fake Stalker to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_VOIDRAY
description: Create a fake Void Ray to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.

description: Create a fake Warp Prism to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.

Ability.name: HALLUCINATION_ZEALOT
description: Create a fake Zealot to serve as a durable scout or to draw enemy fire.

Ability.name: CALLDOWNMULE_CALLDOWNMULE
description: Calls down a MULE, a temporary unit with a timed life of 64 seconds that can mine minerals and repair for the Terran forces.
Calls down a temporary MULE at the target location at the cost of 50 energy that lasts 64 seconds.

Ability.name: GRAVITONBEAM_GRAVITONBEAM
tech_chain: GRAVITONBEAM_GRAVITONBEAM: [Pylon (PROTOSSBUILD_PYLON) -> Gateway (PROTOSSBUILD_GATEWAY) -> CyberneticsCore (PROTOSSBUILD_CYBERNETICSCORE) -> Stargate (PROTOSSBUILD_STARGATE) -> Phoenix (STARGATETRAIN_PHOENIX)] -> GRAVITONBEAM_GRAVITONBEAM (GRAVITONBEAM_GRAVITONBEAM)
description: Lifts a ground unit into the air, disabling it.

Ability.name: SPAWNCHANGELING_SPAWNCHANGELING
tech_chain: SPAWNCHANGELING_SPAWNCHANGELING: [Larva -> Overlord (LARVATRAIN_OVERLORD) -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> Overseer (MORPH_OVERSEER)] -> Changeling (SPAWNCHANGELING_SPAWNCHANGELING)

SPAWNCHANGELING_SPAWNCHANGELING: [Larva -> Overlord (LARVATRAIN_OVERLORD) -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> Overseer (MORPH_OVERSEER) -> OverseerSiegeMode (MORPH_OVERSIGHTMODE)] -> Changeling (SPAWNCHANGELING_SPAWNCHANGELING)

Ability.name: RESEARCH_GLIALREGENERATION
tech_chain: RESEARCH_GLIALREGENERATION: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR)] + [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> RoachWarren (ZERGBUILD_ROACHWARREN)] -> GlialReconstitution (RESEARCH_GLIALREGENERATION)

relations.description: Using RESEARCH_GLIALREGENERATION requires the prerequisite unit or structure Lair.
Executing RESEARCH_GLIALREGENERATION results in Upgrade GlialReconstitution.

Ability.name: RESEARCH_TUNNELINGCLAWS
tech_chain: RESEARCH_TUNNELINGCLAWS: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR)] + [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> RoachWarren (ZERGBUILD_ROACHWARREN)] -> TunnelingClaws (RESEARCH_TUNNELINGCLAWS)

Executing RESEARCH_TUNNELINGCLAWS results in Upgrade TunnelingClaws.

tech_chain: NEURALPARASITE_NEURALPARASITE: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> NeuralParasite (RESEARCH_NEURALPARASITE)] + [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> Infestor (LARVATRAIN_INFESTOR)] -> NEURALPARASITE_NEURALPARASITE (NEURALPARASITE_NEURALPARASITE)

Ability.name: EFFECT_INJECTLARVA

Spawn Larva on Hatcheries by pressing Backspace to cycle through them.

Ability.name: EFFECT_STIM_MARAUDER
tech_chain: EFFECT_STIM_MARAUDER: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS) -> Stimpack (BARRACKSTECHLABRESEARCH_STIMPACK)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS) -> Marauder (BARRACKSTRAIN_MARAUDER)] -> EFFECT_STIM_MARAUDER (EFFECT_STIM_MARAUDER)
description: Increases the movement speed and firing rate by 50% at the cost of 20 HP.
Used on Marauders at the cost of 20hp.

relations.description: Using EFFECT_STIM_MARAUDER requires the upgrade Stimpack.

Ability.name: SUPPLYDROP_SUPPLYDROP
description: Calls down additional supplies which permanently increase a Supply Depot's supply limit by 8 and increases the current and maximum health points to 500, and can only be used once per Supply Depot.

Ability.name: RESEARCH_ANABOLICSYNTHESIS
tech_chain: RESEARCH_ANABOLICSYNTHESIS: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> Hive (UPGRADETOHIVE_HIVE) -> UltraliskCavern (ZERGBUILD_ULTRALISKCAVERN)] -> AnabolicSynthesis (RESEARCH_ANABOLICSYNTHESIS)

relations.description: Executing RESEARCH_ANABOLICSYNTHESIS results in Upgrade AnabolicSynthesis.

Ability.name: RESEARCH_CHITINOUSPLATING
tech_chain: RESEARCH_CHITINOUSPLATING: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> Hive (UPGRADETOHIVE_HIVE) -> UltraliskCavern (ZERGBUILD_ULTRALISKCAVERN)] -> ChitinousPlating (RESEARCH_CHITINOUSPLATING)
description: Increases the armor of all Ultralisks by 2.

relations.description: Executing RESEARCH_CHITINOUSPLATING results in Upgrade ChitinousPlating.

Ability.name: EFFECT_REPAIR_SCV
description: Repair some unit or strcture.
Repairs other buildings and mechanical units, and can be set to autocast so that an idle or patrolling SCV automatically repairs any damaged structure or mechanical unit within close proximity.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_COMMANDCENTER
description: Build a Command Center, the Terran base.

relations.description: Executing TERRANBUILD_COMMANDCENTER results in Unit CommandCenter.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT
description: Build a Supply Depot to increase 8 supplies and unlock Barracks.

relations.description: Executing TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT results in Unit SupplyDepot.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_REFINERY
description: Build a Refinery on a Vespene Geyser.

relations.description: Executing TERRANBUILD_REFINERY results in Unit Refinery.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS
description: Build a Barracks to produce infantry units (Marine, Reaper, Marauder, Ghost).

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS requires the prerequisite unit or structure SupplyDepot.
Executing TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS results in Unit Barracks.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_ENGINEERINGBAY
description: Build an Engineer Bay to unlock Missile Turret, Sensor Tower and Planetary Fortress.

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_ENGINEERINGBAY requires the prerequisite unit or structure CommandCenter.
Executing TERRANBUILD_ENGINEERINGBAY results in Unit EngineeringBay.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_MISSILETURRET
description: Build a Missile Turret to attack flying units.

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_MISSILETURRET requires the prerequisite unit or structure EngineeringBay.
Executing TERRANBUILD_MISSILETURRET results in Unit MissileTurret.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_BUNKER
description: Build a Bunker, a static defense building.

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_BUNKER requires the prerequisite unit or structure Barracks.
Executing TERRANBUILD_BUNKER results in Unit Bunker.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_SENSORTOWER
description: Build a Sensor Tower, which senses any approaching uncloaked, unburrowed enemy units.

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_SENSORTOWER requires the prerequisite unit or structure EngineeringBay.
Executing TERRANBUILD_SENSORTOWER results in Unit SensorTower.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_GHOSTACADEMY
description: Build a Ghost Academy to unlock the Ghost unit.

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_GHOSTACADEMY requires the prerequisite unit or structure Barracks.
Executing TERRANBUILD_GHOSTACADEMY results in Unit GhostAcademy.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_FACTORY
description: Build a Factory to produce mechanical ground units (Hellion, Widow Mine, Siege Tank, Hellbat, Thor) and unlock the Starport and Armory.

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_FACTORY requires the prerequisite unit or structure Barracks.
Executing TERRANBUILD_FACTORY results in Unit Factory.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_STARPORT
description: Build a Starport to produce air units (Viking, Medivac, Banshee, Raven, Battlecruiser).

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_STARPORT requires the prerequisite unit or structure Factory.
Executing TERRANBUILD_STARPORT results in Unit Starport.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_ARMORY
description: Build a Armory to unlock the Hellbat, Thor and higher-level infantry upgrades.

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_ARMORY requires the prerequisite unit or structure Factory.
Executing TERRANBUILD_ARMORY results in Unit Armory.

Ability.name: TERRANBUILD_FUSIONCORE
description: Build a Fusion Core to unlock Battlecruiser.

relations.description: Using TERRANBUILD_FUSIONCORE requires the prerequisite unit or structure Starport.
Executing TERRANBUILD_FUSIONCORE results in Unit FusionCore.

Ability.name: EFFECT_STIM_MARINE
tech_chain: EFFECT_STIM_MARINE: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS) -> Stimpack (BARRACKSTECHLABRESEARCH_STIMPACK)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Marine (BARRACKSTRAIN_MARINE)] -> EFFECT_STIM_MARINE (EFFECT_STIM_MARINE)
description: Increases the movement speed and firing rate by 50% at the cost of 10 HP.
Used on Marines at the cost of 10hp.

relations.description: Using EFFECT_STIM_MARINE requires the upgrade Stimpack.

Ability.name: BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_GHOST
tech_chain: BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_GHOST: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> GhostAcademy (TERRANBUILD_GHOSTACADEMY) -> PersonalCloaking (RESEARCH_PERSONALCLOAKING)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS) -> GhostAcademy (TERRANBUILD_GHOSTACADEMY) -> Ghost (BARRACKSTRAIN_GHOST)] -> BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_GHOST (BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_GHOST)
description: The Ghost becomes invisible until it runs out of energy or the player cancels Cloak.
Renders the Ghost invisible to enemies without detection.

relations.description: Using BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_GHOST requires the upgrade PersonalCloaking.

Ability.name: MEDIVACHEAL_HEAL
tech_chain: MEDIVACHEAL_HEAL: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> Medivac (STARPORTTRAIN_MEDIVAC)] -> MEDIVACHEAL_HEAL (MEDIVACHEAL_HEAL)
description: Enables a Medivac to heal nearby injured units.
This spell works on friendly biological units in team games.

Ability.name: SIEGEMODE_SIEGEMODE
tech_chain: SIEGEMODE_SIEGEMODE: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_FACTORY) -> SiegeTank (FACTORYTRAIN_SIEGETANK)] -> SiegeTankSieged (SIEGEMODE_SIEGEMODE)
description: Transforms a Siege Tank into Siege Mode, increasing its attack range and damage but preventing movement.

relations.description: Executing SIEGEMODE_SIEGEMODE results in Unit SiegeTankSieged.

Ability.name: UNSIEGE_UNSIEGE
description: Converts a Siege Tank back from Siege Mode to its regular mode.

relations.description: Executing UNSIEGE_UNSIEGE results in Unit SiegeTank.

Ability.name: BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_BANSHEE
tech_chain: BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_BANSHEE: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS) -> BansheeCloak (RESEARCH_BANSHEECLOAKINGFIELD)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT) -> Banshee (STARPORTTRAIN_BANSHEE)] -> BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_BANSHEE (BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_BANSHEE)
description: The Benshee becomes invisible until it runs out of energy or the player cancels Cloak.

relations.description: Using BEHAVIOR_CLOAKON_BANSHEE requires the upgrade BansheeCloak.

Ability.name: LOAD_MEDIVAC
description: Load a unit into a Medivac.

Ability.name: SCANNERSWEEP_SCAN
description: Scanner Sweep scans a target location on the map revealing cloaked, burrowed, and hallucinated units temporarily.

Ability.name: YAMATO_YAMATOGUN
tech_chain: YAMATO_YAMATOGUN: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> FusionCore (TERRANBUILD_FUSIONCORE) -> BattlecruiserEnableSpecializations (RESEARCH_BATTLECRUISERWEAPONREFIT)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT) -> FusionCore (TERRANBUILD_FUSIONCORE) -> Battlecruiser (STARPORTTRAIN_BATTLECRUISER)] -> YAMATO_YAMATOGUN (YAMATO_YAMATOGUN)
description: Blasts a target with a devastating plasma cannon, causing 240 damage.

relations.description: Using YAMATO_YAMATOGUN requires the upgrade BattlecruiserEnableSpecializations.

Ability.name: MORPH_VIKINGASSAULTMODE
tech_chain: MORPH_VIKINGASSAULTMODE: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> VikingFighter (STARPORTTRAIN_VIKINGFIGHTER)] -> VikingAssault (MORPH_VIKINGASSAULTMODE)
description: Transforms the Viking into Assault Mode where it becomes a Ground unit with a ground attack.

relations.description: Executing MORPH_VIKINGASSAULTMODE results in Unit VikingAssault.

Ability.name: MORPH_VIKINGFIGHTERMODE
description: Transforms the Viking into Fighter Mode where it becomes an Air unit with an anti-air attack.

relations.description: Executing MORPH_VIKINGFIGHTERMODE results in Unit VikingFighter.

Ability.name: LOAD_BUNKER
description: Load a unit into a Bunker.

Ability.name: UNLOADALL_BUNKER
description: Unload all units from a Bunker.

Ability.name: LIFT_COMMANDCENTER
description: The Command Center can no longer lift off once it has transformed into a Planetary Fortress.

relations.description: Executing LIFT_COMMANDCENTER results in Unit CommandCenterFlying.
Executing LAND_COMMANDCENTER results in Unit CommandCenter.

Ability.name: BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS
description: Unlocks Marauder and is a requirement for Ghost.

relations.description: Executing BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS results in Unit BarracksTechLab.

Ability.name: BUILD_REACTOR_BARRACKS
description: Allows structure create 2 units simultaneously.

relations.description: Executing BUILD_REACTOR_BARRACKS results in Unit BarracksReactor.
Executing LIFT_BARRACKS results in Unit BarracksFlying.

Ability.name: BUILD_TECHLAB_FACTORY
description: Unlocks Siege Tank and is a requirement for Thor.

relations.description: Executing BUILD_TECHLAB_FACTORY results in Unit FactoryTechLab.

Ability.name: BUILD_REACTOR_FACTORY
description: Allows structure create 2 units simultaneously.

relations.description: Executing BUILD_REACTOR_FACTORY results in Unit FactoryReactor.
Executing LIFT_FACTORY results in Unit FactoryFlying.

Ability.name: BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT
tech_chain: BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> StarportFlying (LIFT_STARPORT)] -> StarportTechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT)
description: Unlocks Raven and Banshee and is a requirement for Battlecruiser.

relations.description: Executing BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT results in Unit StarportTechLab.

Ability.name: BUILD_REACTOR_STARPORT
tech_chain: BUILD_REACTOR_STARPORT: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> StarportFlying (LIFT_STARPORT)] -> StarportReactor (BUILD_REACTOR_STARPORT)
description: Allows structure create 2 units simultaneously.

relations.description: Executing BUILD_REACTOR_STARPORT results in Unit StarportReactor.
Executing LIFT_STARPORT results in Unit StarportFlying.
Executing LAND_FACTORY results in Unit Factory.
Executing LAND_STARPORT results in Unit Starport.

Ability.name: COMMANDCENTERTRAIN_SCV
description: Train a SCV, a Terran worker.

relations.description: Executing COMMANDCENTERTRAIN_SCV results in Unit SCV.
Executing LAND_BARRACKS results in Unit Barracks.
Executing MORPH_SUPPLYDEPOT_LOWER results in Unit SupplyDepotLowered.
Executing MORPH_SUPPLYDEPOT_RAISE results in Unit SupplyDepot.

Ability.name: BARRACKSTRAIN_MARINE
description: Train a Marine, a versatile, low-cost infantry unit that provides reliable ranged damage and forms the backbone of most Terran armies.

relations.description: Executing BARRACKSTRAIN_MARINE results in Unit Marine.

Ability.name: BARRACKSTRAIN_REAPER
description: Train a Reaper, a fast, mobile infantry unit designed for early-game harassment and scouting, capable of jumping cliffs with its jetpack.

relations.description: Executing BARRACKSTRAIN_REAPER results in Unit Reaper.

Ability.name: BARRACKSTRAIN_GHOST
tech_chain: BARRACKSTRAIN_GHOST: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> GhostAcademy (TERRANBUILD_GHOSTACADEMY)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS)] -> Ghost (BARRACKSTRAIN_GHOST)
description: Train a Ghost, a stealthy special forces unit that can snipe high-value targets, deploy tactical nukes, and use cloaking for strategic advantage.

relations.description: Using BARRACKSTRAIN_GHOST requires the prerequisite unit or structure GhostAcademy.
Using BARRACKSTRAIN_GHOST requires the prerequisite unit or structure TechLab.
Executing BARRACKSTRAIN_GHOST results in Unit Ghost.

Ability.name: BARRACKSTRAIN_MARAUDER
description: Train a Marauder, a heavily armored infantry unit that excels against armored targets with its concussive grenades, complementing Marine forces.

relations.description: Using BARRACKSTRAIN_MARAUDER requires the prerequisite unit or structure TechLab.
Executing BARRACKSTRAIN_MARAUDER results in Unit Marauder.

Ability.name: FACTORYTRAIN_SIEGETANK
description: Train a Siege Tank, a transforming artillery unit that bombards ground targets from long range with devastating area-of-effect damage.

relations.description: Using FACTORYTRAIN_SIEGETANK requires the prerequisite unit or structure TechLab.
Executing FACTORYTRAIN_SIEGETANK results in Unit SiegeTank.

Ability.name: FACTORYTRAIN_THOR
tech_chain: FACTORYTRAIN_THOR: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_FACTORY)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Armory (TERRANBUILD_ARMORY)] -> Thor (FACTORYTRAIN_THOR)
description: Train a Thor, a heavily-armored combat walker that excels against massive ground units and light air fleets.

relations.description: Using FACTORYTRAIN_THOR requires the prerequisite unit or structure TechLab.
Using FACTORYTRAIN_THOR requires the prerequisite unit or structure Armory.
Executing FACTORYTRAIN_THOR results in Unit Thor.

Ability.name: FACTORYTRAIN_HELLION
tech_chain: FACTORYTRAIN_HELLION: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Armory (TERRANBUILD_ARMORY) -> HellionTank (TRAIN_HELLBAT)] -> Hellion (MORPH_HELLION)
description: Train a Hellion, a fast harassment vehicle that incinerates light ground units in a line.

relations.description: Executing FACTORYTRAIN_HELLION results in Unit Hellion.

Ability.name: TRAIN_HELLBAT
tech_chain: TRAIN_HELLBAT: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Armory (TERRANBUILD_ARMORY)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Hellion (FACTORYTRAIN_HELLION)] -> HellionTank (MORPH_HELLBAT)
description: Train a Hellbat, a tough, short-ranged walker that incinerates masses of light units with its wide flame attack.

relations.description: Using TRAIN_HELLBAT requires the prerequisite unit or structure Armory.
Executing TRAIN_HELLBAT results in Unit HellionTank.

Ability.name: TRAIN_CYCLONE
description: Train a Cyclone, locking onto a single air or ground target to deal immense damage while moving to stay at range.

relations.description: Using TRAIN_CYCLONE requires the prerequisite unit or structure TechLab.
Executing TRAIN_CYCLONE results in Unit Cyclone.

Ability.name: FACTORYTRAIN_WIDOWMINE
description: Train a Widow Mine, a burrowing trap that launches a devastating missile at the first enemy ground or air unit to cross its path.

relations.description: Executing FACTORYTRAIN_WIDOWMINE results in Unit WidowMine.

Ability.name: STARPORTTRAIN_MEDIVAC
description: Train a Medivac, a flying support unit that heals biological forces and transports them across the battlefield.

relations.description: Executing STARPORTTRAIN_MEDIVAC results in Unit Medivac.

Ability.name: STARPORTTRAIN_BANSHEE
tech_chain: STARPORTTRAIN_BANSHEE: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT)] -> Banshee (STARPORTTRAIN_BANSHEE)
description: Train a Banshee, a cloaked air unit that excels at surprise attacks against ground targets.

relations.description: Using STARPORTTRAIN_BANSHEE requires the prerequisite unit or structure TechLab.
Executing STARPORTTRAIN_BANSHEE results in Unit Banshee.

Ability.name: STARPORTTRAIN_RAVEN
tech_chain: STARPORTTRAIN_RAVEN: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT)] -> Raven (STARPORTTRAIN_RAVEN)
description: Train a Raven, a flying spellcaster that disrupts enemy armies with debilitating abilities and provides detection.

relations.description: Using STARPORTTRAIN_RAVEN requires the prerequisite unit or structure TechLab.
Executing STARPORTTRAIN_RAVEN results in Unit Raven.

Ability.name: STARPORTTRAIN_BATTLECRUISER
tech_chain: STARPORTTRAIN_BATTLECRUISER: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> FusionCore (TERRANBUILD_FUSIONCORE)] -> Battlecruiser (STARPORTTRAIN_BATTLECRUISER)
description: Train a Battle Cruiser, a powerful capital ship that can teleport across the map and unleash a devastating single-target blast.

relations.description: Using STARPORTTRAIN_BATTLECRUISER requires the prerequisite unit or structure TechLab.
Using STARPORTTRAIN_BATTLECRUISER requires the prerequisite unit or structure FusionCore.
Executing STARPORTTRAIN_BATTLECRUISER results in Unit Battlecruiser.

Ability.name: STARPORTTRAIN_VIKINGFIGHTER
description: Train a Viking Fighter, a transforming fighter that dominates the skies in air mode and provides ground support in assault mode.

relations.description: Executing STARPORTTRAIN_VIKINGFIGHTER results in Unit VikingFighter.

Ability.name: STARPORTTRAIN_LIBERATOR
description: Train a Liberator, an air unit that transforms into a stationary platform to lockdown and destroy ground targets in a designated area.

relations.description: Executing STARPORTTRAIN_LIBERATOR results in Unit Liberator.

Ability.name: RESEARCH_HISECAUTOTRACKING
description: Adds +1 attack range to Auto-Turrets, Missile Turrets, and Planetary Fortresses.

relations.description: Executing RESEARCH_HISECAUTOTRACKING results in Upgrade HiSecAutoTracking.

Ability.name: RESEARCH_TERRANSTRUCTUREARMORUPGRADE

relations.description: Executing RESEARCH_TERRANSTRUCTUREARMORUPGRADE results in Upgrade TerranBuildingArmor.

relations.description: Executing ENGINEERINGBAYRESEARCH_TERRANINFANTRYWEAPONSLEVEL1 results in Upgrade TerranInfantryWeaponsLevel1.

tech_chain: ENGINEERINGBAYRESEARCH_TERRANINFANTRYARMORLEVEL3: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Armory (TERRANBUILD_ARMORY) -> EngineeringBay (TERRANBUILD_ENGINEERINGBAY) -> TerranInfantryArmorsLevel1 (ENGINEERINGBAYRESEARCH_TERRANINFANTRYARMORLEVEL1) -> TerranInfantryArmorsLevel2 (ENGINEERINGBAYRESEARCH_TERRANINFANTRYARMORLEVEL2)] + [EngineeringBay (TERRANBUILD_ENGINEERINGBAY)] -> TerranInfantryArmorsLevel3 (ENGINEERINGBAYRESEARCH_TERRANINFANTRYARMORLEVEL3)

tech_chain: BUILD_NUKE: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> GhostAcademy (TERRANBUILD_GHOSTACADEMY)] -> BUILD_NUKE (BUILD_NUKE)

Ability.name: RESEARCH_COMBATSHIELD
description: Every Marine gains a Combat Shield that gives 10 hitpoints.

relations.description: Using RESEARCH_COMBATSHIELD requires the prerequisite unit or structure Barracks.

Ability.name: RESEARCH_CONCUSSIVESHELLS

relations.description: Using RESEARCH_CONCUSSIVESHELLS requires the prerequisite unit or structure Barracks.
Executing RESEARCH_CONCUSSIVESHELLS results in Upgrade PunisherGrenades.

tech_chain: RESEARCH_INFERNALPREIGNITER: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS)] -> HighCapacityBarrels (RESEARCH_INFERNALPREIGNITER)

Executing RESEARCH_INFERNALPREIGNITER results in Upgrade HighCapacityBarrels.

tech_chain: RESEARCH_DRILLINGCLAWS: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY)] + [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_BARRACKS)] -> DrillClaws (RESEARCH_DRILLINGCLAWS)

The Overlord generates creep in a discreet location to allow important buildings to be built underneath, but the building will start to die if the Overlord moves away or is killed.

Unit.name: Colossus
tech_chain: Colossus: [Pylon (PROTOSSBUILD_PYLON) -> Gateway (PROTOSSBUILD_GATEWAY) -> CyberneticsCore (PROTOSSBUILD_CYBERNETICSCORE) -> RoboticsFacility (PROTOSSBUILD_ROBOTICSFACILITY) -> RoboticsBay (PROTOSSBUILD_ROBOTICSBAY)] -> Colossus (ROBOTICSFACILITYTRAIN_COLOSSUS)
description: The Colossus ( plural -lossi, -lossuses) is a massive Protoss battle strider that towers over all other ground units on the battlefield. It is built at the Robotics Facility , but requires the presence of a Robotics Bay to be unlocked. Its long-range dual Thermal Lances target units in a wide linear area of effect , perpendicular to the Colossus's direction of fire. This makes the Colossus a powerful siege unit that can quickly destroy an enemy's ground forces.

Colossi have massive legs and enormous height, which can both be an advantage and disadvantage. Firstly, the height allows anti-air attacks to hit it; care must be taken with Colossi to make sure units like Vikings or Corruptors do not pick them off in battle as they are relatively fragile units for their cost. Secondly, its weapon comes with a base range of 7, and the Extended Thermal Lance upgrade extends it to an impressive attack range of 9, which allows it to attack from behind the main Protoss army. Thirdly, the legs grant them the ability to traverse cliffs and provide vision of high ground areas, as well as stacking over other units in the player's army. However unlike smaller ranged units, if a Colossus is on the high ground and attacks enemy units on the low ground, the enemy gains temporary vision of the Colossus and can return fire. This revealing mechanic does put a limit on their ability to exploit high ground, but can still be useful for engaging short ranged units. Overall, care must be taken to account for these unique characteristics, but these also help make the colossus a powerful unit.

The Colossus is a heavy support unit that deals large amounts of damage over a moderately sized area (2.8 width, 0.3 depth). The massive battle strider is capable of obliterating large amounts of light ground forces within seconds.

The Legacy of the Void version of the Colossus does less damage in general than earlier versions of the unit. In return for +1 base range (though the same total with Extended Thermal Lance) and greater attack speed, its base damage was reduced. It does bonus damage against light units, and thus can be effective against massed light units such as Marines, Zerglings, and Zealots.

Because of their damage reduction and other new units in the meta, the Immortal has succeeded the Colossi as the premier Anchor unit of the Robotics Facility. Versus Zerg, the Ravager 's Corrosive Bile destroys forcefields, removing the most important tool when supporting Colossus armies, and Lurkers can stall these walkers long enough to tech to the many counters against them. Alongside that, Lurkers rip the standard complementary gateway unit to Colossi, the Stalker , which would otherwise normally provide anti-armor and anti-air. In PvP Disruptors have become the primary form of splash damage because they have the ability to blow up Protoss units in less hits, are cheaper than Archons , and don't need any upgrades to achieve damage. Therefore it is only against Terran now where Colossi are most suitable, providing consistent splash that Marine micro cannot mitigate.

Researching Extended Thermal Lance remains standard in all match-ups if Colossi are to be built, as the upgrade grants it longer range than most other ground units, allowing it to fire from a safe distance. It is only outranged by the Terran Siege Tank in its immobile Siege Mode and the Zerg Lurker with Seismic Spines . Along with Cliff Walk, this allows the Colossus to kite ground units using the terrain to its advantage. Most ground units will not be able to pursue correctly managed Colossi without taking high amounts of damage or simply dying.

The Colossus's main weakness is its additional vulnerability to air attacks. Vikings , Corruptors , and Tempests deal bonus damage to Massive or Armored units, both of which apply to the Colossus. As Air units can easily flank the Protoss army, they may circumvent the Protoss anti-air and destroy the powerful Colossi without risk. A Colossus-heavy army needs to be bolstered with appropriate numbers of anti-air support, in order to combat aerial threats.

There are a few units with different anti-air and anti-ground weapons. Of these, the Tempest , Queen , and Infested Terran will prefer to attack the Colossus with their AA weapon, and the Battlecruiser attacks with its AG weapon. If the Thor engages with its AA weapon (which is longer-range), it will continue attacking with it, but if it begins to engage at low enough range, it prefers its AG weapon.

The Colossus synergizes well with Sentries and Stalkers . Force fields can be used both to block off and to entrap enemy ground units (allowing for maximized splash damage) while Stalkers provide anti-air support and act as a meat shield (alongside Zealots ); the Blink ability may also increase the Stalkers' damage absorption. With its height, the Colossus is able to reveal areas atop cliffs, enabling allied units to fire upwards on to high ground, as well as enabling Stalkers to Blink to targeted locations, which would otherwise be inaccesible. One Colossus fills a Warp Prism .
As a rule of thumb, it is possible to sustain continuous Colossi production out of double Robotics Facilities per mining base, so long as no Chrono Boost is used.
Colossi are not typically used in PvP matches in Legacy of the Void. Protoss players typically use Immortal, Archon, Chargelot (IAC) or Blink/Disruptor compositions instead of Colossus-based armies.

Being the most consistent source of splash damage in PvT, Colossi are not susceptible to micro techniques Terran players can use against the other main splash damage options, Psi Storm and Disruptors . Colossi are good against Marine -heavy compositions owing to their bonus against Light units. They are however very weak against large masses of Marauders , both because Colossi have no bonus damage against them and because Marauders deal bonus damage against the Armored Colossi.

Colossi can be effective if the Zerg decides to make Hydralisk , Zergling or Baneling -heavy armies. Against Armored units such as Roaches , they are not as cost-effective as their heavy-fire counterpart, the Immortal .

Use of Colossus-based armies in PvZ is very limited as they require a significant investment to bring onto the field. While they can be effective at a certain point, they only remain so while the Zerg makes nothing but Lings, Banes or Hydras or a combination thereof. They also fare quite badly facing Zerg Hive tech. Vipers are useful, Abducting Colossi right into the Zerg forces, killing them before they can deal any real damage. Ultralisks with their high armor (and their large size making them less vlnerable to splash damage) take low amounts of damage from Colossi, hence necessitating the use of Immortals. Corruptors work as well as Vikings, with the Colossus's trait of anti-air vulnerability. Brood Lords are also deadly as they outrange Colossi and deal large amounts of ground damage. Lurkers , especially with Adaptive Talons, are also an option against Colossi, as they slow the advance of a Colossus-based army if it lacks Detection.

The Colossus hard counters Marines because it deals bonus damage against light units, making it highly effective against massed groups like Marines.
The Colossus hard counters Zerglings because it deals bonus damage against light units, which includes Zerglings.
The Colossus hard counters the Zealot because it deals bonus damage against light units, which includes Zealots.
The Colossus soft counters the Siege Tank by using its range and terrain to kite ground units, despite being outranged by the Siege Tank.
The Colossus soft counters the LurkerMP because it is only outranged by the Zerg Lurker with Seismic Spines, implying a tactical disadvantage.
The Colossus synergizes with the Sentry because the Sentry's force fields and guardian shield protect the Colossus while it deals area-of-effect damage.

The Colossus synergizes with Stalkers because the Colossus deals area-of-effect damage to ground units while Stalkers provide mobile anti-air support and focus fire, creating a balanced and effective Protoss army composition.
Colossus synergizes with Zealot because Zealots act as a meat shield, protecting the Colossus while it deals damage.
A Colossus can garrison in a Warp Prism because one Colossus fills the transport capacity of a Warp Prism.
Colossi are effective against Hydralisk-heavy armies due to their area-of-effect attacks, making them a soft counter.
Colossi are effective against Zergling-heavy armies due to their area-of-effect damage, making them a soft counter.
Colossi are effective against Baneling-heavy armies due to their range and area-of-effect damage, making them a soft counter.
The Colossus hard counters Banelings because it murders the ling bane composition, as stated in the evidence.
Colossi protect Tempests by deterring enemy infantry, creating a synergistic relationship where the Colossus safeguards the Tempest from ground threats.
Colossi with Extended Thermal Lances are effective against Marauders because they can rip through them, as noted in the evidence.
The Colossus soft counters Broodlings because its splash damage can quickly destroy large numbers of them.
Colossi are a significant threat to Hydralisks because they can burn down the army, making them a hard counter.

Unit.name: TechLab
description: The Tech Lab is an add-on for the Barracks , Factory and Starport that unlocks new tech and upgrades. For example, a Barracks with a Tech Lab can produce Marauder and Ghost units.

Players can exchange the building attached to a Tech Lab to achieve tech swaps, tech rushes or early unit production. For instance, when doing a fast Banshee build, players can simultaneously build a Tech Lab on a Factory while also building the Starport. When the Tech Lab finishes, the player can float the Factory away and land the Starport next to the now free Tech Lab. Now the player can build Banshees immediately, without having to wait for a Tech Lab to build.
Unlocks Marauders and is a requirement for Ghosts .
Unlocks Siege Tanks and is a requirement for Thors .
Unlocks Ravens and Banshees and is a requirement for Battlecruisers .

Unit.name: Reactor
description: The Reactor is one of the two Terran add-on buildings , which can be built from and attached to Barracks , Factories and Starports . It enables the attached building to build two units simultaneously and increases the size of its build queue from 5 to 8.
Because a building can only have one add-on attached to it at a time, the effect of Reactors is restricted to units not requiring Tech Labs:

As Reactors are usually cheaper than building a second production facility of the same kind, they are widely used when building several units of any of the types listed above. Chances are that any building not used for building Tech Lab units ends up with a Reactor sooner or later.

Exchanging the building attached to a Reactor using lift off is a frequent occurrence. For one, it is useful for tech switching . Further, less important production buildings, such as perhaps a Factory in a bio -heavy build, can accelerate unit production by building add-ons for the more important buildings. In particular in the early game, many builds aim for the mass production of certain Factory or Starport units as soon as possible.

Reactor can transform or morph into FactoryReactor.
Reactor can transform or morph into StarportReactor.

Unit.name: InfestorTerran
tech_chain: InfestorTerran: [Larva -> Burrow (RESEARCH_BURROW)] + [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> Infestor (LARVATRAIN_INFESTOR)] -> InfestorTerran (BURROWDOWN_INFESTORTERRAN)

InfestorTerran: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> InfestationPit (ZERGBUILD_INFESTATIONPIT) -> Infestor (LARVATRAIN_INFESTOR) -> Burrow (RESEARCH_BURROW) -> InfestorBurrowed (BURROWDOWN_INFESTOR)] -> InfestorTerran (BURROWUP_INFESTORTERRAN)
description: The Infested Terran was a light , ground Zerg unit that was spawned by an Infestor using its Infested Terran spell . The spell, and with it the unit, were removed in Patch 4.11.0 in November 2019. They were replaced by Microbial Shroud .

Infested Terrans are generally used in conjunction with Fungal Growth to pick off enemy drops, or deal extra damage to an army as an energy dump. Infested Terrans can be used as a form of worker harassment. As of Patch 16, Infested Terrans can be cast while the Infestor is burrowed and therefore hidden. Infestors in groups of 6 or more can tunnel into an enemy base and spawn multiple Infested Terrans to snipe key buildings like Robotics Bays or Command Centers .

Protoss units are fairly resistant to Infested Terran; however, they can be used to add anti-air damage for a Zerg player who opted not to go for Hydralisks . They are especially effective against Interceptors as well as Phoenixes .

Versus biomech builds, Infestors can cast Infested Terran on top of an infantry ball, and sieged Siege Tanks will splash their own units. Infested Terrans are also very useful to shoot down incoming or retreating Medivac dropships, commonly with the help of Fungal Growth to keep them in place.

Infested Terrans can be used around a ranged Zerg army to block Ultralisks . The eggs work especially well because of their low aggro, high HP, and good spread to avoid splash. They can also be used to deal with Mutalisks by using Fungal Growth to pin them, allowing the Infested Terrans time to hatch and kill them.

InfestorTerran hard counters RoboticsBay because it spawns multiple Infested Terrans to snipe key buildings like Robotics Bays.
InfestorTerran hard counters CommandCenter by spawning multiple Infested Terrans to snipe and destroy key buildings like Command Centers.
InfestorTerran hard counters Interceptors because they are especially effective against them, as explicitly stated in the evidence.
InfestorTerran hard counters Phoenixes because they are especially effective against them, as stated in the evidence.
InfestorTerran soft counters SiegeTankSieged because sieged Siege Tanks will splash their own units, and Infested Terrans cause friendly fire splash damage for Siege Tanks.
InfestorTerran soft counters Medivac because Infested Terrans are useful to shoot down incoming or retreating Medivac dropships.
Infested Terrans can block Ultralisks, providing a tactical soft counter by hindering the melee unit's movement.
InfestorTerran can spawn or temporarily create InfestedTerransEgg.
InfestorTerran soft counters Mutalisk because they can be used to deal with Mutalisks through tactical setup.

Unit.name: BanelingCocoon
description: The Cocoon is the unit created from any morphable Zerg unit after it has begun mutating into another unit.
The Cocoon from a morphing Larva is extremely durable, with 200 hit points and 10 armor.
The Cocoon from a Zergling morphing to a Baneling has 50 life and 2 armor.
Overseer and Broodlord Cocoons both have 200 life and 2 armor.
The Cocoon from a Ravager has 100 life and 5 armor.
The Cocoon from a Lurker has 100 life and 1 armor

When a Cocoon is created, it will be as damaged as the unit that created it, based on percentage of total life. For example, a Zergling with 85% life (30 hit points) will make a Cocoon with 85% life (43 hit points). If the Cocoon is canceled, the same conversion occurs once again, and the unit will have the same percentage of life the Cocoon had.

If the mutation is successful, the new unit will spawn with 100% life, regardless of the state the Cocoon was in. The unit is considered a brand new unit, so if effects such as Concussive Shells or Corruption are applied to the Cocoon, the new unit will not have those effects once it spawns. If the Cocoon is canceled, the effects will still be present.

All Cocoons that morph from units (i.e. from Zerglings , Ravagers , Lurkers , Corruptors , and Overlords ) benefit from their respective Ground Carapace or Flyer Carapace upgrades. Cocoons from a Larva do not benefit from these upgrades.

Canceling a Cocoon from a Larva restores 100% of the invested minerals and gas but the used Larva will be killed. Canceling a Cocoon from a Zergling , Ravager , Lurker , Corruptor , or Overlord restores 75% of the invested minerals and gas.

Infested Swarm Egg , an egg form of Infested Terran , transfers damage to its hatched form. For example, if the egg is at 35HP at the time of hatching (50% life), the produced Infested Terran will only have 25HP (50% life).

relations.description: BanelingCocoon has access to the ability RALLY_BUILDING.
BanelingCocoon has access to the ability SMART.

Unit.name: Baneling
tech_chain: Baneling: [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> BanelingNest (ZERGBUILD_BANELINGNEST)] + [Larva -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Zergling (LARVATRAIN_ZERGLING)] -> Baneling (MORPHTOBANELING_BANELING)
description: The Baneling is a suicide bomber unit morphed from Zerglings after a Baneling Nest has been constructed. Similar to the Infested Terran from Brood War, the Baneling is a suicidal unit that deals significant splash damage.

Banelings are a very effective unit against Light units due to their high damage bonuses in combination with splash damage. Correctly using a group of Banelings can deal massive damage to a hostile army and allow other units to clean up any remaining army much more easily. Because of their low survivability, getting Banelings to successfully hit groups of enemies is key. There are many options that can help a player use Banelings effectively:

Try to surround and attack, pull back a little and attack again. You don't want to waste Banelings on tanky units such as Marauders . You want to hit light units, so be wary of good players who will use these Armored units to shield their Light units.

Banelings deal a lot of damage against structures, dealing significantly more damage that scales well with upgrades but they are very cost-ineffective. There are several things to note about Baneling damage vs. structures:

With the nerfs to Baneling damage vs non- light units, as well as their losing the ability to one-shot Probes , Banelings are rarely seen in army compositions anymore, most players now preferring Lurkers instead. With that said, Banelings are still used in some situations for harassment and mid-game timings.

Against Terran bio, Banelings see extensive use. They are primarily being used to kill or, at the very least, flush Marines out from the Terran bio-ball. In combination with Zerglings , Banelings can be devastating as they force the bio to split, increasing the surface area for the Zerglings. However, Banelings are very vulnerable to Widow Mines and Siege Tanks that are properly target fired, being one shot by the splash damage of both units.

Banelings see much less use against mech due to their aforementioned weakness to Siege Tanks . Despite that, they are sometimes used in high-momentum army compositions to destroy Planetary Fortresses , albeit with very poor cost efficiency.

Most games of ZvZ are characterized by the early game Ling-Bane phase, as both players try to out-micro each other to either end the game or secure a faster third base, centering around the Baneling's ability to instantly kill all Zerglings in its splash damage range.

While many games will continue on into the Roach - Ravager phase, increasingly more often one player will choose to go with Mutalisks . After this they can choose to transition into Ultra - Ling -Bane- Viper . This composition depends on high-momentum, being out-traded by Hydra - Lurker - Viper due to the Baneling's vulnerability to Lurkers, it being critical to deny the other player's mining.

When attacking with Banelings, it is important to know whether one's Baneling count is high enough, as attacking with too few Banelings to breach the defenses often results in a bad trade . This is especially true when "Baneling busting" a wall or sniping a Planetary Fortress .

Banelings deal splash damage which is highly effective against clumped melee units like Zealots.
Banelings are ineffective against tanky units like Marauders, making Marauders a soft counter to Banelings.
Banelings hard counter Probes because they can one-shot them, as evidenced by the text mentioning this ability and its subsequent nerf.
Banelings are explicitly used to kill or flush Marines out from the Terran bio-ball, indicating a hard counter relationship.
Banelings synergize with Zerglings by forcing bio units to split, increasing the surface area for Zerglings to attack effectively.
Banelings are used to destroy Planetary Fortresses, implying a hard counter role despite poor cost efficiency.
The Baneling hard counters the Zergling because its splash damage can instantly kill all Zerglings within range.
Banelings synergize with Mutalisks as part of the Ultra-Ling-Bane-Viper composition transition, providing ground support while Mutalisks handle aerial threats.
Banelings synergize with Vipers as implied by the transition into an Ultra-Ling-Bane-Viper composition, where Vipers support Banelings by disabling or clumping enemies for effective attacks.
A single Baneling can one shot Larvae, indicating a decisive hard counter.
Banelings synergize with Infestors as they are part of a combined arms composition often used together with Zerglings and Ultralisks.

Unit.name: Mothership
tech_chain: Mothership: [Pylon (PROTOSSBUILD_PYLON) -> Gateway (PROTOSSBUILD_GATEWAY) -> CyberneticsCore (PROTOSSBUILD_CYBERNETICSCORE) -> Stargate (PROTOSSBUILD_STARGATE) -> FleetBeacon (PROTOSSBUILD_FLEETBEACON)] + [Nexus (PROTOSSBUILD_NEXUS)] -> Mothership (NEXUSTRAINMOTHERSHIP_MOTHERSHIP)
description: The Mothership is the most powerful unit in the Protoss arsenal: Once a Fleet Beacon has been built, it may be warped in at a Nexus . Because of its extensive capabilities, the Mothership has a unique restriction: only one can be deployed by each player at a time.

Although it does have an attack in the Purifier Beam, the primary use of the Mothership is its powerful support abilities. The Mothership has three spells, Cloaking Field , Mass Recall and Time Warp . The Mass Recall spell teleports player units in the target area to the Mothership after 4 seconds, just like the Nexus ' Strategic Recall spell, but without any cooldown. The Time Warp spell slows down the units and structures in a targeted area. Last but not least, the ability Cloaking Field cloaks all nearby friendly units and buildings for 20 seconds. While these abilities are powerful and potentially game-changing, the Mothership is very expensive, quite slow, takes a long time to warp in, and is only available at the end of a long tech path. Thus, the Mothership is generally only seen in late game compositions, although specialized builds can produce it earlier.

There used to be very few builds tailored to incorporate the Mothership, and was often used as a cheese or an embarrassment tactic, such as the Mothership Rush All In as first played by HuK in 2010, which heavily relied on surprise to be effective.
However, as players have more likelihood of getting expansions and a large income, the Mothership has become an integral part of many Protoss late game strategies.
The Mothership was rarely used when the Mothership Core was still in the game, mirroring all of its abilities except for the cloaking field.

A less common way to employ the mothership is a build that utilizes the cloaking ability of the mothership. The Mothership usually follows a Phoenix harass, as at that point the Stargate is built and there are required Phoenixes out. After that, the Protoss using this strategy gets a Fleet Beacon and a Mothership, also getting 1 or more Observers to accompany the army. When the confrontation with the other player occurs, your Observers spot his, and since they are likely the only air unit in the army, the Phoenix will kill the opponent's Observer. Then it is simply a matter of using the cloaking on the Mothership to keep your army alive, and keeping the Mothership alive as best you can. It should be noted that this build is very difficult to effectively pull off, since the Mothership takes so long to tech to and build, and it is almost always more practical to simply produce more units and try to get a stronger army or economy.

The Mothership's cloaking ability can be a powerful asset against Terran; Terran players are heavily dependent on Scans for detection, and Ravens can be usually be picked off relatively easily in the later-stages game. This forces Terran players to use Orbital Command Energy, which limits MULE production and can actually run out in longer or multiple engagements. However, as Vikings and EMPs are usually available for Terran as well as large numbers of Orbital Commands, Mothership play is usually ineffective against Terran.

Fortunately the unit does have a nice little niche in a certain scenario of Tempest vs BC battles. Nexus Recall is tied to a global cooldown meaning it can't be used again until 85 seconds. A Mothership serves as a backup recall for when the player needs to recall in a pinch. This can be seen in this game of Creator vs TY

With the Mothership's heroic tag it is impossible for Infestors to target it with Neural Parasite , the only real threat was getting abducted into the range of Corruptors by a Viper , but Patch 5.0.14 removed the ability to abduct Motherships.

The Mothership synergizes with the Phoenix because the strategy utilizes Phoenix harass followed by Mothership deployment.
The Mothership synergizes with the Observer because Observers accompany the army to support the Mothership strategy by spotting enemy detectors.
The Mothership creates an environment where Ravens are vulnerable, allowing them to be picked off relatively easily, which implies a soft counter dynamic.
The Mothership synergizes with the Tempest by providing a backup recall in niche Tempest vs Battlecruiser scenarios.
The Mothership soft counters LocustMP by using cloaking to deny vision, making it a useful tool against Locust waves.
The Mothership synergizes with the Carrier by using Time Warp to slow down Corruptors, protecting the Carriers from dive-bombing attacks.

Unit.name: PointDefenseDrone
description: The Point Defense Drone (or PDD for short) was a light-air Terran unit removed in the 4.0.0 patch, it is deployed by a Raven at the cost of 100 energy . The Drone is immobile and cannot attack, but will negate specific enemy projectiles near itself, protecting allied units. The Drone spawns with 200 energy, which allows for up to 20 attacks to be negated. Point Defense Drones can be constructed within 3 range of their host Raven . Once deployed, the Raven can leave the area, and the Drone will remain for its 14-second duration.

In general, visible projectiles can be shot down, but not energy streams (e.g. from Void Rays), invisible bullets (e.g. from Marines), or blobs of goo (from Roaches). The PDD is therefore ineffective against the ground attacks of Vikings and Thors, despite these units being listed; only their anti-air attacks feature visible projectiles to be shot down.

The additional armor is generally not researched if the Terran player intends to use Ravens primarily for PDDs. It provides marginal benefit for PDD usage at the cost of additional gas on an already gas-heavy build (Each Raven costs 200 gas).
The Point Defense Drone can defend missile attacks from the following units:

Unit.name: Changeling
tech_chain: Changeling: [Larva -> Overlord (LARVATRAIN_OVERLORD) -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> Overseer (MORPH_OVERSEER)] -> Changeling (SPAWNCHANGELING_SPAWNCHANGELING)

Changeling: [Larva -> Overlord (LARVATRAIN_OVERLORD) -> SpawningPool (ZERGBUILD_SPAWNINGPOOL) -> Lair (UPGRADETOLAIR_LAIR) -> Overseer (MORPH_OVERSEER) -> OverseerSiegeMode (MORPH_OVERSIGHTMODE)] -> Changeling (SPAWNCHANGELING_SPAWNCHANGELING)
description: The Changeling is a temporary Zerg unit spawned by an Overseer that lasts for 107 seconds. The Changeling spawns as a Zerg-looking unit, but upon seeing an enemy unit or building it will automatically transform into the basic unit of that enemy's race ( Marine , Zergling , or Zealot ). If the enemy player has researched Combat Shield for Marines or Metabolic Boost for Zerglings, then the Changeling will also copy the upgraded models for those units. The Changeling will not gain the new model if the enemy player researches one of those upgrades after the Changeling has already morphed.

Once disguised, the Changeling takes on the enemy's own color and will no longer be automatically attacked by the enemy's units, allowing it to infiltrate enemy territory unsuspected. Changelings cannot be discerned as impostors by any form of detection , nor will the secondary effects of EMP Round reveal their true allegiance. Likewise, a Sensor Tower 's Radar ability will not identify the Changeling as hostile after it has morphed. However, if the opponent selects the Changeling, they will be unable to give it orders (since it is not theirs) and the word "Changeling" will appear in place of the unit type that it appears to be. In the event that an opponent does not spot a Changeling joining their army, it will often be revealed when they notice that it doesn't have a selection circle around it when they box their army. This can be slightly mitigated by ordering the Changeling to follow the unit it is disguised as within an army, so that it won't be noticed when the army is moved.

Disguised Changelings can only be killed by manually forcing units to attack it (right-clicking on it will issue an Attack command) or waiting for its timer to elapse. Note that the Changeling's Disguise ability is only triggered within a range of 12 and only if vision is provided for it, as the Changeling itself features a sight range of just 8. Units that are "hidden" to the Changeling (e.g. unspotted units on a cliff or cloaked units) will easily dispatch of it in its native form. Also, note that Siege Tanks can attack up to a distance of 13 and thus can automatically kill an unmorphed Changeling before it can get in range to disguise itself.

Some properties of the Changeling change after it disguises itself. Changelings disguised as Zerglings will move at the speed of a slow Zergling and get a speed multiplier from Creep , though they do not benefit from any player's Metabolic Boost upgrade even if they appear to have wings. Changelings disguised as Marines or Zealots have the sight range of the copied unit, increasing their sight to 9. Finally, Changelings disguised as Zealots will appear to lose their shields to an EMP Round , but will begin regaining them immediately at a rate of 0.5 points per second. This is in contrast to real Protoss units, which will not begin regaining their shields for 10 seconds and will regain them at 2 points per second.

The Changeling is most often used to keep track of the opponent's main army, and is usually ordered to follow one of the enemy units (preferably of the same kind). You then get constant updates on the composition and position of their army, so that making unit counters, flanking and harassment are all much easier. These are very effective scouts against an uninformed opponent.
Changelings are also good for gaining temporary control over a Xel'Naga Tower as they do not cost any supply .

It is possible to create a pseudo Force Field using a group of Changelings to block off a ramp. By using multiple Overseers , it is possible to spawn a large mass of Changelings. Putting these on a ramp on H old will require the enemy to manually target all the Changelings. [1]
Changelings work very well against Protoss since Zealots are normally part of any army and will be hard to spot, allowing for a lot of scouting.
Changelings can be more effective against a Zerg player because the Zerglings they morph into are easier to hide and harder to target.

Unit.name: CommandCenter
description: The Command Center is the town center of a Terran base. It is the building where SCVs are created and where they bring their collected Minerals and Vespene Gas. The Command Center also has the ability to lift off of its current position, to relocate to a new location. Finally, the Command Center can load up to 5 SCVs (Can be upgraded to 10) to either protect or take them with it to a new expansion.

Unit.name: SupplyDepot
description: The Supply Depot is a building constructed by Terran for the purpose of providing supply. Each Supply Depot raises the Terran's supply cap by eight. Supply Depots are also used for strategic purposes, typically for walling . Another use for them is to be placed near other Terran buildings to create obstructions that enemy units must walk around, giving Terran time to out micro his opponent. Supply Depots can also be lowered to allow passage over them. This is useful when letting units in and out of walls and for making movement around a Terran base easier when large amounts of depots have been constructed.

Unit.name: Refinery
description: The Refinery is the gas extraction building for Terran. When constructed on top of a Vespene Gas Geyser, it allows the Terran player to collect Vespene Gas by sending SCVs into it. SCVs collect 4 gas at a time and return it to the nearest Command Center until the geyser is depleted. Refineries can only be built on Vespene Gas Geysers and are the only way a Terran player can mine Vespene Gas.

Unit.name: Barracks
description: The Barracks (Rax for short) is the infantry production building for Terran; allowing them to build Marines , Marauders , Reapers and Ghosts . It is commonly used as part of a Terran wall-in and can be lifted off the ground and landed elsewhere after it has been constructed.
In mech -based play, a Barracks will often be lifted to serve as a heavily armored scout or to spot for Siege Tanks .

Unit.name: EngineeringBay
description: The Engineering Bay (commonly abbreviated as E-Bay or Engi-Bay) is a structure in the Terran Tech Tree which requires a Command Center before it can be built. The Engineering Bay is the research center for Infantry Weapons , Infantry Armor , Hi-Sec Auto Tracking and Neosteel Armor . It also unlocks the Missile Turret , the Sensor Tower and the Planetary Fortress so building an Engineering Bay is common even if infantry units are not used.
Unlike its Brood War counterpart , the Engineering Bay cannot Lift off in StarCraft II.

Unit.name: MissileTurret
description: The Missile Turret (or Turret for short) is the Terran's anti-air static defense as well as one of the Terran's detectors . Missile Turrets require an Engineering Bay in order to be constructed, which also has upgrades for the Missile Turret: Hi-Sec Auto Tracking and Building Armor .

The Missile Turret's attack is about as powerful as four Marines . The twin attack (12x2) makes it less effective against high armor targets such as Battlecruisers but despite this setback, Missile Turrets still deal more DPS per cost than the Photon Cannon or Spore Crawler . Although they cannot out-range Carriers , the Missile Turret's high damage and endurance is still very potent for clearing out Interceptors .

A few well placed Missile Turrets can fend off Banshee harass in the early game. Since a Missile Turret can detect farther than it can shoot, it is still useful when the Cloaked Banshee is out of range. A small group of stimmed Marines can run up to a detected Banshee and shoot it down if the opposing player does not pull back immediately.
Turrets are also commonly used in conjunction with Siege Tanks to fortify a position. When deployed in the field, they can assist Vikings in the air battle or detect cloaked Banshees and Ghosts .

A handful of Missile Turrets can effectively hold off large numbers of Mutalisks and is recommended for protecting your vulnerable SCVs . When under attack, your SCVs can repair the Missile Turrets, increasing its longevity. However, this guarantees that the Mutalisk's Glaive Worm attack will bounce from the Missile Turret onto the SCVs.
The Missile Turret is also able to spot burrowed Infestors and Roaches , which can prevent a potentially disastrous sneak attack on your mineral lines.

Missile Turrets can minimize damage taken from Dark Templar , and can also be used to ward off Phoenix harass. They are decent against uncharged, lone Void Ray attacks, but fall extremely quickly to multiple charged Void Rays. They are a strong choice to defend from Oracle harass, placed in close proximity to mineral lines.

Missile Turrets are detectors that hard counter cloaked units like Dark Templar, greatly limiting access to the base with such units.
Missile Turrets are explicitly stated to destroy Interceptor squadrons quickly, making them a hard counter.
The Missile Turret's high damage and endurance make it effective at clearing out Interceptors, establishing a soft counter relationship.
Missile Turrets soft counter Banshees because a few well placed turrets can fend off Banshee harass.
Missile Turrets synergize with Siege Tanks because they are commonly used together to fortify a position.
Missile Turrets synergize with Vikings by providing support in air combat, assisting them during air battles.
Missile Turrets hard counter Banshees because they detect cloaked Banshees, negating their primary defense.
Missile Turrets hard counter Ghosts because they can detect cloaked Ghosts, negating their primary defense.
Missile Turrets hard counter Mutalisks because they can effectively hold off large numbers of them, as stated in the evidence.
Missile Turrets soft counter Phoenix because they can be used to ward off Phoenix harass through anti-air attacks.
Missile Turrets soft counter Void Rays because they are decent against uncharged, lone Void Ray attacks, though effectiveness depends on specific states.
Missile Turrets are a strong choice to defend from Oracle harass and serve as the primary static defense against Oracle detection and harassment.

Unit.name: SensorTower
description: The Sensor Tower senses - but does not detect - any approaching uncloaked, unburrowed enemy units and displays them as red exclamation marks in the fog of war . An opponent can see the Sensor Tower's radius as a circle on their minimap .

The Sensor Tower can be very useful on certain maps, such as Scrap Station and Antiga Shipyard because it can detect drops early and also warn of imminent Mutalisk harassment and allow the player time to move their forces to defend.
The Sensor Tower does not give units such as tanks with larger range than their vision the ability to fire on sensed targets.

relations.description: SensorTower has access to the ability SALVAGEEFFECT_SALVAGE.

Unit.name: GhostAcademy
description: The Ghost Academy unlocks the Ghost unit, which can be built from the Barracks . The Ghost Academy can research upgrades for Ghosts and can arm itself with a Nuke that can be deployed under a Ghost's command, however each Ghost Academy may only hold one Nuke at a time.

Unit.name: Factory
description: The Factory is one of the advanced Terran tech buildings that allows Terran to build Hellions , Hellbats , Widow Mines , Siege Tanks , and Thors . The Factory requires a Barracks before it can be built. The Factory can be lifted off and landed elsewhere after it has been constructed.
A Factory is also required before you are able to build a Starport , thus it is a key step in the Terran Tech Tree . It is also required to build a Nuke .

If you choose to go for a mechanical unit mix towards mid/endgame, then you'll need to build a few Factories to churn out the mechanical units. With the addition of the Widow Mine and the Hellbat , Mech-Play is a lot more viable. One opener for Mech that has been seen is a push with Marines and Widow Mines to deny the natural. Another successful harassment-method with Mech is Hellbat drops in TvZ and TvT. Hellbats can also replace Marines in TvT, but you will be giving up air control. They are, however, a much better meat shield.

Alternatively, if you choose to go for a primarily Bio -based army the Factory can be used as a scout. The Starport is built as soon the Factory is finished and the Factory is Lifted from the ground and flown in the direction of the enemies base. Since the Starport tech has already started there is no downside to losing the Factory if you are not using its units for your composition. Also, the Factory has a large number of Hitpoints and 1 armor meaning it can withstand a large degree of punishment from enemy units while scouting. A side effect of using this Factory for scouting is that the Factory is landed inside the enemies base and Hellions can be produced and used for extras scouting or harassment. Again, due to the Factory's hitpoints, in the early stages of the game it can get Hellions onto the field even if the Factory is being attacked. Dustin Browder in an interview with Team Liquid, mentioned that this was not an intended usage of the Factory. [ 1 ]
The Factory is one of the key parts in the 1/1/1 build. Another successful build is a Factory opener with Hellion harass.

Unit.name: Starport
description: The Starport is the Terran production building for air units. It requires a Factory before it can be built. It can be lifted off.

Initially, the Starport can only produce Vikings , Medivacs , or Liberators , one at a time -- however, like Barracks and Factories , attaching add-ons increase this capability. Attaching a Reactor allows the Starport to produce two of these units at a time while attaching a Tech Lab allows a Starport to produce Banshees , Ravens and Battlecruisers (the latter also requiring a Fusion Core ).

A common technique is to start both your Starport and a Reactor on your Factory as soon as the Factory is complete. Since both take 50 seconds to complete, you can swap the Factory and Starport upon completion (resulting in a Reactored Starport) allowing you to immediately "double-pump" Medivacs or Vikings. Similarly, this technique can be used to get faster cloaked Banshees by constructing a Tech Lab on your factory.

Unit.name: Armory
description: The Armory is a Terran tech building that requires a Factory to be built. It unlocks the Thor and Hellbat units at the Factory and the Level 2 & 3 Infantry upgrades at the Engineering Bay . It is also where upgrades for Factory and Starport units are researched. The existence of an Armory will grant burrowed Widow Mines invisibility while reloading.

Unit.name: FusionCore
description: The Fusion Core is a 3.5 tier building that allows the Battlecruiser to be built at the Starport .

The Fusion Core requires a Starport to be built and can research Weapon Refit , the upgrade for Battlecruisers to unlock Yamato Cannon , as well as the Advanced Ballistics upgrade for the Liberator , and the Caduceus Reactor upgrade for the Medivac .

Unit.name: AutoTurret
tech_chain: AutoTurret: [SupplyDepot (TERRANBUILD_SUPPLYDEPOT) -> Barracks (TERRANBUILD_BARRACKS) -> Factory (TERRANBUILD_FACTORY) -> Starport (TERRANBUILD_STARPORT) -> TechLab (BUILD_TECHLAB_STARPORT) -> Raven (STARPORTTRAIN_RAVEN)] -> AutoTurret (BUILDAUTOTURRET_AUTOTURRET)
description: The Auto-Turret is a temporary and immobile structure deployed by the Raven at the cost of 50 energy. It automatically attacks nearby air and ground targets that come within its range.

Once deployed, the Raven can leave the area, and the Turret will remain until it is killed or its duration expires (7.9 seconds). At expiration, the Auto-Turret would have dealt a maximum of 234 damage before armor.

Auto-Turrets see limited usage in competitive StarCraft. This is primarily because the Raven 's other spells see more attention in competitive play, leaving the Auto-Turret as an energy dump after Seeker Missiles and Point Defense Drones have been cast. Despite this, Auto-Turrets retain specific uses in all match ups.

Aside from their use as mobile vision/detection, Ravens are less commonplace in TvP , which in turn limits Auto-Turret usage. However, they still retain their protective use, especially when part of a Marine / Siege Tank push. Because Feedback is capable of instantly destroying a Raven or Point Defense Drone that is providing vision for Siege Tanks , Auto-Turrets can be used as spotters instead. A major downfall of Auto-Turret usage against Protoss are the units high shield health, coupled with quick shield regeneration. Where an Auto-Turret can damage a Zerg unit and have the damage remain for minutes, Protoss shields often endure the entire attack and negate its effects shortly thereafter.

Though not as common as just using Point Defense Drones in this match up, the auto-turrets can be used to bomb Siege Tanks and make Tanks splash each other. The Auto-Turret can survive 3 Tank attacks or 2 fully upgraded attacks, which is enough to kill a standard Siege Tank. On top of this, they will deal minor additional damage with their standard attack. With the Point Defense Drone not being able to intercept Marine fire, an Auto-Turret can pose as an HP buffer, especially with its armor upgraded from 1 to 3. Because the PDD intercepts Marauders ' grenades, it is usually a better choice if the opponent builds large numbers of Marauders.

Auto-Turrets are extremely good against Zerg opponents that have Banelings in their army, as dropping one down amongst Banelings can draw their fire to it, causing multiple Banelings to suicide on the Auto-Turret. Since most Terrans opt to launch a Seeker Missile first, one Auto-Turret can be placed with the left over energy. Aside from drawing Baneling fire, Auto-Turrets are rather powerful in their own right as a support unit. An Auto-Turret right in front of a player's army will distract a hostile, Zergling -heavy composition, especially with its armor upgraded from 1 to 3. By throwing a few Auto-Turrets down, Terrans can gain a slight advantage and possibly win a fight that they would otherwise have lost.

An Auto-Turret distracts a hostile, Zergling-heavy composition, providing a tactical advantage through distraction.

Unit.name: SiegeTank
description: The long-ranged Siege Tank is a Mechanical unit with high damage, particularly versus Armored like Roaches and Stalkers . Against masses of smaller units, the Siege Tank can switch to the stationary Siege Mode to deal splash damage from longer range. It is built from a Factory with an attached Tech Lab .

The Siege Tank is a powerful tool for securing specific tactically important areas of the map, but somewhat immobile compared to other Terran units such as the Marine and other biological units, particularly due to its transformation time. They are also a reliable source of ground splash damage for Terrans.
